name: NASANeoWs_get_feed
description: Get a list of near-Earth asteroids (NEOs) approaching Earth within a date range using NASA's NeoWs (Near Earth Object Web Service). Returns asteroid names, estimated diameters, close approach data (distance, velocity), and whether they are potentially hazardous. Essential for planetary defense research, asteroid tracking, and space situational awareness. Date range limited to 7 days. Uses free DEMO_KEY (rate limited to 30 req/hour).

start_date.description: Start date in YYYY-MM-DD format (e.g., '2025-01-15'). Feed covers up to 7 days from this date.

end_date.description: End date in YYYY-MM-DD format (e.g., '2025-01-16'). Must be within 7 days of start_date. Defaults to start_date + 7 days if omitted.

element_count.description: Total number of NEOs in the date range

near_earth_objects.description: NEOs grouped by date (key is YYYY-MM-DD). Each date maps to an array of asteroid objects with name, estimated_diameter, is_potentially_hazardous_asteroid, and close_approach_data.

name: NASANeoWs_get_asteroid
description: Get detailed information about a specific near-Earth asteroid by its SPK-ID (Small-Body Perturber ID) from NASA's NeoWs API. Returns orbital data, estimated diameter ranges (km/m/miles/feet), close approach history and predictions, absolute magnitude, and hazard classification. Useful for tracking specific asteroids, studying orbital mechanics, and assessing impact risk. Uses free DEMO_KEY.

nasa_jpl_url.description: URL to NASA JPL page for this asteroid

is_potentially_hazardous_asteroid.description: Whether this asteroid is classified as potentially hazardous

estimated_diameter.description: Estimated diameter in various units (kilometers, meters, miles, feet) with min/max ranges

close_approach_data.description: List of close approaches with date, relative velocity, and miss distance

orbital_data.description: Orbital parameters including eccentricity, semi-major axis, inclination, orbital period

is_sentry_object.description: Whether monitored by NASA Sentry impact monitoring system

name: NASANeoWs_browse_asteroids
description: Browse the full catalog of near-Earth asteroids from NASA's NeoWs API. Returns paginated results of all known NEOs with basic data including name, estimated diameter, hazard classification, and close approach information. Useful for building comprehensive asteroid databases, statistical analysis of NEO populations, and systematic surveys. Uses free DEMO_KEY.

page.description: Page number (0-indexed). Default: 0. Each page returns 20 asteroids.

size.description: Number of results per page (default 20, max 20)

near_earth_objects.description: Array of asteroid objects with id, name, estimated_diameter, is_potentially_hazardous_asteroid, close_approach_data, orbital_data

page.description: Pagination info with size, total_elements, total_pages, number
